summary: Shared hardcoded Telegram bot token enables publisher-observable remote shell channel
details: stella-cli/telegram-bot.mjs hardcodes a single Telegram bot token (`BOT_TOKEN = "8923551485:AAF..."`) that is baked into every published copy of the package. When a user invokes the CLI subcommand `/tg`, the package begins polling that shared Telegram bot; messages received from an authenticated Telegram user are passed to `execSync` invoking the Stella CLI with the message text as a prompt, and the Stella CLI itself exposes full filesystem, git, and shell tooling. Authentication for the remote channel is a 4-digit numeric admin code (brute-force space 10^4) plus a hardcoded shipped premium activation code (`PREMIUM_CODE = "10102013"`). Because the bot token is publisher-controlled and identical for every installation, whoever holds that token — the package author, or anyone who obtains it from the tarball — sits on the same remote-control channel as every user who ever runs `/tg`, and can attempt the 4-digit admin auth to reach `execSync` on the installer's machine via the agent's shell tools. Trigger is user-invoked rather than install-time, but the design provides a structural covert remote-access position that installers did not opt into.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
